items.correct: When a sentence or phrase runs over from one line of poetry to the next without a punctuated pause, creating flow or surprise across the line break.
items.misconception: Enjambment is when a poem has very long lines.
items.random_wrong: Enjambment is when a poem uses no punctuation at all.
items.why: Enjambment is about syntax crossing the line break, not line length or punctuation in general. Students miss that it is about the relationship between grammar and lineation.

items.correct: A line of five iambs (unstressed-stressed syllable pairs), creating a ten-syllable line with the rhythm da-DUM da-DUM da-DUM da-DUM da-DUM.
items.misconception: Iambic pentameter is any line with ten syllables.
items.random_wrong: Iambic pentameter is when every line rhymes.
items.why: Syllable count alone doesn't make iambic pentameter — the stress pattern is essential. And iambic pentameter is usually unrhymed (blank verse) in Shakespeare's plays.

items.correct: Subtle hints or clues earlier in a text that anticipate later events, making the resolution feel both surprising and inevitable.
items.misconception: Foreshadowing is when a character predicts the future.
items.random_wrong: Foreshadowing is the shadow a character casts in a scene.
items.why: Foreshadowing works on the reader, not within the story world. A character's prediction is a plot event — foreshadowing is an authorial technique embedded in detail and tone.

items.q: Where does the dagger soliloquy occur?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Act 2. “Act 1” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: What philosophy does Macbeth express in the 'tomorrow' speech?
items.why: Correct answer: Nihilism. “Optimism” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many times does Macbeth repeat 'tomorrow' in the famous line?
items.why: Exact answer: 3. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
